title: OpenAI Launches GPT-Rosalind for Life Sciences Research
summary: OpenAI released GPT-Rosalind, a specialized AI model for biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine, named after DNA researcher Rosalind Franklin.
content: OpenAI has announced GPT-Rosalind, a new AI model specifically tailored for life sciences, including biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. Named after British scientist Rosalind Franklin, the model understands deep connections in chemistry, protein engineering, and genomics, and can access over 50 scientific tools and data sources via a new Life Sciences plugin for Codex. In internal tests, GPT-Rosalind surpasses predecessors GPT-5, GPT-5.2, and GPT-5.4 in areas such as chemistry, protein biochemistry, and phylogenetics, achieving a pass rate of 0.751 on the bioinformatics benchmark BixBench — the highest among available models. The model is available as a research preview within ChatGPT, Codex, and the API for qualified customers, with early partners including Amgen, Moderna, the Allen Institute, and Thermo Fisher Scientific. OpenAI describes GPT-Rosalind as the first in a planned series of domain-specific models, with future versions expected to better support long-term, tool-intensive research workflows.

title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7 with Tripled Vision and Stronger Agentic Coding
summary: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.7, its most capable generally available model, with tripled image resolution support, stronger agentic software engineering, and a new ultra-high reasoning effort level.
content: Anthropic has launched Claude Opus 4.7, its strongest coding model to date and the newest flagship in its Opus lineup. The model handles complex, long-running agentic tasks with far less supervision than its predecessor Opus 4.6, and uniquely verifies its own outputs before reporting back to users. Vision capability has tripled: Opus 4.7 now supports images up to approximately 3.75 megapixels, making it significantly more reliable for high-resolution image analysis tasks including those required by Claude Design. Developers gain a new 'xhigh' effort level between 'high' and 'max', offering finer control over the reasoning-versus-latency tradeoff and enabling better performance in legal, scientific, and other expert domains. Two major Claude Code features ship alongside the model: the '/ultrareview' slash command for on-demand deep code review, and improved long-horizon task management that lets Claude maintain context across extended engineering projects.

title: Anthropic Launches Claude Design, Challenging Figma and Canva
summary: Anthropic released Claude Design in research preview, a visual creation tool powered by Claude Opus 4.7 that generates polished designs, prototypes, and presentations from conversational prompts.
content: Anthropic launched Claude Design, a new product from its Anthropic Labs division that allows users to create polished visual work — including designs, interactive prototypes, slide decks, one-pagers, and marketing collateral — through conversational prompts and fine-grained editing controls. The release is available immediately in research preview to all paid Claude subscribers (Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise) and represents Anthropic's most aggressive expansion beyond its core language model business into the application layer historically dominated by Figma, Adobe, and Canva. Claude Design is powered by Claude Opus 4.7, whose tripled image resolution and improved instruction-following make it uniquely capable for visual design workflows. The timing is significant: Anthropic reached roughly $20 billion in annualized revenue in early March 2026, and launching Claude Design alongside Opus 4.7 signals the company's intent to move from foundation model provider to full-stack AI application platform. The dual-track approach — Claude Opus 4.7 for broad public use and Claude Mythos Preview locked behind vetted access — is described as unprecedented in the AI industry.

title: Adobe Launches Firefly AI Assistant to Orchestrate Creative Workflows
summary: Adobe unveiled its Firefly AI Assistant, a conversational agent that automatically executes multi-step creative tasks across Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, and other Creative Cloud apps.
content: Adobe announced the Firefly AI Assistant, a conversational interface that allows creators to describe desired edits in plain language while the AI orchestrates and executes complex, multi-step workflows across Creative Cloud apps including Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Express, Illustrator, and more. The assistant builds on Project Moonlight, previewed at Adobe MAX last year, and will enter public beta in the coming weeks. Adobe describes the launch as 'a fundamental shift in how creative work is done,' removing skill barriers by letting users type natural-language instructions such as 'retouch this image' or 'resize this for social media' instead of navigating professional editing tools. The Firefly AI Assistant will learn user preferences over time — preferred tools, workflows, and aesthetic choices — to deliver more personalized and consistent results, with creators retaining full control over whether this feature is enabled. Adobe also confirmed the assistant will integrate with third-party AI apps including Anthropic's Claude, allowing users outside of Creative Cloud to access Adobe tools through external AI platforms.

title: Google DeepMind Releases Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 with Enhanced Spatial Reasoning
summary: Google DeepMind launched Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, a major upgrade to its robotics AI model featuring improved spatial reasoning, a new instrument-reading capability developed with Boston Dynamics, and stronger safety compliance.
content: Google DeepMind introduced Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, a reasoning-first model designed to give robots unprecedented precision in understanding their physical environments. The model specializes in visual and spatial reasoning, task planning, and success detection, acting as a high-level reasoning layer that can natively call tools like Google Search or any third-party user-defined functions. A newly unlocked capability — instrument reading — enables robots to interpret complex analog gauges and sight glasses, developed through collaboration with Boston Dynamics for industrial facility inspection using Spot robots. Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 shows significant benchmark improvements over both its predecessor ER 1.5 and Gemini 3.0 Flash, particularly in pointing, counting, and multi-view success detection across multiple simultaneous camera streams. The model is also described as DeepMind's safest robotics release to date, with substantially improved adherence to physical safety constraints, and is available now to developers via the Gemini API and Google AI Studio.

title: White House Releases National AI Policy Framework Seeking Federal Preemption of State Laws
summary: The Trump administration's National Policy Framework for AI recommends that Congress establish a unified federal standard, preempt conflicting state AI laws, and oppose creating a new federal AI regulator.
content: On March 20, 2026, the White House released a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence containing sweeping legislative recommendations intended to establish a coherent, nationally unified approach to AI governance. The framework's most consequential provision recommends that Congress preempt state AI laws that 'impose undue burdens,' aiming to replace the current patchwork of state regulations with a single minimally burdensome national standard — a move that could render laws like Colorado's AI Act or California's automated decision-making rules unenforceable. The framework explicitly opposes creating any new federal rulemaking body to regulate AI, instead calling for oversight to remain with existing regulatory agencies and industry-led standards. Other key recommendations include child safety protections with age-verification requirements for AI platforms, intellectual property protections for digital replicas and creator likenesses, and regulatory sandboxes to accelerate AI development. States retain authority over generally applicable consumer protection laws, zoning for AI infrastructure, and their own use of AI in public services, but the framework signals that federal preemption will be the dominant legislative battleground in the months ahead.
